name: C++
description: A high-performance systems language used in game engines, embedded systems, OS development, and competitive programming.

lessons.title: Hello World & Compilation
lessons.content: C++ programs must be compiled before running. Every program needs a main() function. #include imports libraries. 'cout' outputs text to the console and '<<' is the stream insertion operator.

exercises.question: Which object is used to print output in C++?
hints: It stands for 'character output' and is in <iostream>

lessons.title: Variables & Primitive Types
lessons.content: C++ has strict static types. Common types: int (integer), double (decimal), bool (true/false), char (single character), string (text, requires <string>). All variables must be declared with a type.

exercises.question: Which C++ type stores a decimal number?
hints: It has double precision floating point

lessons.title: Pointers & References
lessons.content: Pointers store memory addresses. The & operator gets an address; the * operator dereferences it. References are aliases for variables. Pointers and references are fundamental to C++ memory management.
lessons.code_example:
int x = 10;
int* ptr = &x;   // ptr holds the address of x
*ptr = 20;       // change x through the pointer
cout << x;       // 20

int& ref = x;    // ref is an alias for x
ref = 30;
cout << x;       // 30

exercises.question: What does the * operator do when used on a pointer?
options: Dereferences — accesses the value at the address
exercises.answer: Dereferences — accesses the value at the address

lessons.title: Classes & OOP
lessons.content: C++ supports OOP with classes. A class has private data members and public methods. The constructor initializes the object. Use '.' to access public members on an object.

exercises.question: What access specifier makes class members accessible from outside the class?
hints: private = internal only; public = accessible everywhere
